title: The Italian Game
summary: Classical, principled, still played at the top

steps.title: The Italian
steps.text: The Italian Game begins with the most principled first move: e4. We'll build up to Bc4, eyeing f7, the classical recipe.

steps.hint: Push the king's pawn two squares.

steps.prompt: Now 2. Nf3, attacking the e5 pawn.
steps.hint: Knight to f3 — every master's go-to.

steps.prompt: 3. Bc4 — stake your claim on f7.
steps.hint: Bishop to c4 enters the Italian Game proper.
